text.title: Scaffold project structure and key patterns
text.summary: Canonical directory tree the scaffolding phase emits, plus the three load-bearing code patterns every outlet uses: SDK options split (structured vs agent), prompt split convention, and review loop.

section.title: Top-level directories
section.p: Every outlet has the same first-level layout under workspace/projects/<outlet>/.

list.item: CLAUDE.md — always points at AGENTS.md.
AGENTS.md — project overview for agents.
pipeline/ — Python pipeline package (the core engine).
admin/ — optional Flask admin panel for review-mode outlets.
gatsby/ — static site source (Gatsby 5).
scripts/ — shell entry points for cron and manual operations.
content/ — markdown articles consumed by Gatsby.
state/ — runtime artifacts (plans, teasers, runs, logs, summaries.json, editor_notes.md).
tests/ — pytest suite.

section.title: pipeline/ subtree
section.p: The Python package layout is identical across outlets so muscle memory transfers.

list.item: __init__.py, __main__.py — module entry (python3 -m pipeline).
cli.py — argument parsing and mode dispatch.
config.py — all configuration constants.
context.py — PipelineContext dataclass passed between stages.
sdk.py — Claude Agent SDK wrapper exposing structured_query and agent_query.
json_repair.py — defensive JSON parsing.
feeds.py — RSS feed fetcher (when sources include RSS).
image_gen.py — OpenAI image generation wrapper.
telegram.py — Telegram Bot API wrapper.
run_report.py — execution reporting.
exceptions.py — custom exception types.
schemas/ — JSON schema per stage (editorial_plan, generation, review, tg_post, digest).
prompts/ — Jinja2 builder plus templates/ folder; templates use the system / split / task convention.
stages/ — one file per stage, numbered s0_, s1_, ... matching the chosen complexity level.
modes/ — generate (morning batch), publish (mechanical Telegram publish), digest (evening digest).

section.title: gatsby/ subtree
section.p: Static site identical across outlets except for theme and content.

list.item: gatsby-config.js, gatsby-node.js, package.json.
src/pages/index.js — home.
src/templates/article.js — article page rendered from markdown.
src/components/layout.js, seo.js — layout and SEO head.
src/styles/global.css — theme.
deploy.sh — SSH deploy script.
static/images/ — generated images served as static assets.

section.title: admin/ subtree
section.p: Present only when automation level is "Generate plus review before publish".

list.item: app.py — Flask admin panel with login, queue view, approve/reject actions.

section.title: scripts/ subtree
section.p: Shell entry points used by media-manager cron jobs and manual operators.

list.item: run-pipeline.sh — cron entry point that activates venv and dispatches modes.
send_post.py — direct Telegram sender used by the publish mode.
manage_state.py — clear, inspect, and rebuild state files.

section.title: state/ subtree
section.p: Runtime artifacts. Never committed in full; the structure is committed empty.

list.item: plans/ — daily editorial plans (one JSON per day).
teasers/ — pre-generated Telegram captions waiting to publish.
runs/ — pipeline run reports.
logs/ — pipeline logs.
summaries.json — article index used for deduplication.
editor_notes.md — operator wishes injected into the next plan.

section.title: SDK pattern
section.p: Two ClaudeAgentOptions presets, both with permission_mode bypassPermissions.

list.item: structured_query — no tools, JSON output. Disables Read, Glob, Grep, Bash, Write, Edit, WebSearch, WebFetch, Agent, TodoWrite, TodoRead. max_turns 1. cwd /tmp so the project CLAUDE.md does not load.
agent_query — with tools, free-text output. Allows WebSearch, WebFetch, Read, Glob. cwd is the project root.

section.title: Prompt split convention
section.p: Every Jinja2 prompt template emits one rendered string with a literal split marker. The SDK wrapper splits on the marker, treats the first half as the system prompt, the second half as the user prompt.

list.item: Top half: a system tag carrying role, rules, and context.
Marker: the literal token ===SPLIT=== on its own line.
Bottom half: a task tag carrying specific instructions, data, and the JSON schema.

section.title: Review loop pattern
section.p: Every Standard or Full pipeline runs a bounded review loop with a minimum-one-revision rule.

list.item: The loop iterates up to MAX_REVIEW_CYCLES (typically 3).
Each iteration runs structured_query against review_schema; if approved AND at least one revision has already happened, the loop exits.
Otherwise structured_query runs revise_schema and replaces ctx.article with the revised text.
The minimum-one-revision rule prevents the reviewer from rubber-stamping the first draft.

section.title: Batch generation pattern
section.p: The generate mode runs editorial planning once, then iterates per-topic.

list.item: editorial_plan returns ten to twelve topics for the day.
collect loads RSS items and the existing slug list.
For each topic: skip if already written, otherwise research, generate, review_loop, tg_caption, image, save, mark_written.
Single deploy at the end after all articles land in content/.

section.title: Telegram text-link pattern
section.p: Captions append the link as inline HTML, never as an inline keyboard button. An invisible-link prefix forces the OG preview to load above the caption.

list.item: Caption body ends with an HTML anchor labelled with a forward arrow.
An invisible non-breaking-space link is prepended for OG preview.
Telegram payload uses parse_mode HTML and link_preview_options with prefer_large_media true and show_above_text true.
